learning_outcomes.description: Critique the foundations of innovation performance and the balance between flexibility and efficiency in dynamic environments.
Collaborate effectively within diverse teams to develop informed strategic innovation decisions.
Appraise the ethical implications and strategic risks associated with embedding innovation capabilities in modern organisations.
Construct a professional portfolio of personal attributes and skills to drive future innovation leadership.

assessments.due_date:
Team Based Activity #1 - 28/08/2026

Team Based Activity #2 - 4/09/2026

Team Based Activity #3 - 11/09/2026

Team Based Activity #4 - 18/09/2026
assessments.task_description:
This task is strictly secure and screen-free. This assessment comprises 5 graded components in total (5 x 8% = 40%): 4 team-based content scores (8% x 4 = 32%) derived from weekly activities between weeks 5 and 8, and 1 individual pitch score (8%). For each weekly activity, students will prepare by reviewing course content and a designated case study or sector. During the class session, students will be randomly assigned to teams of four to collaborate, apply the designated course content, and prepare a short pitch (3–5 minutes). To assess the ability to collaborate effectively within diverse groups, teams will be reshuffled randomly for each activity.

Please Note: The presentation will be recorded for marking purposes per UQ Policy.
assessments.submission_guidelines: This is an in-class assessment. For each weekly activity (Weeks 5-8), students will collaborate in randomly assigned teams to prepare and deliver a short pitch during the class session. There is no individual file submission required on Blackboard for the weekly activities, as grading is completed based on in-class performance. Detailed instructions for the individual components will be provided on Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: As this assessment consists of live, in-class activities and team-based pitches, late submissions cannot be accepted. Students who miss a session without an approved extension or alternative arrangement will receive a mark of zero for that week's component.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description: The team project focuses on writing a consulting report that appraises the history of the focal organisation and the pathways available to building innovation capabilities. Students will use theoretical frameworks to assist with analysing and interpreting the organisation and recommend actionable steps to build a more innovative organisation. A peer assessment is required to be submitted via a Peer Assessment tool in Blackboard.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submit via the Team Assessment link in Blackboard - only ONE report per team.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description: This individual assessment task focuses on constructing a personal reflective portfolio. Students will reflect on their learning journey throughout the course, capturing their skill growth, personal attributes, and the application of innovation leadership theories and frameworks.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submit your individual reflection via the assessment link in Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.
